question: [File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/record/RecordFileDownloader.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: getSidecar()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the conditional archive at lines 151-153 — where sidecar bytes are archived only if isWriteFiles() is true — to cause a scenario where the sidecar is processed and its staking state changes are applied, but the bytes are not persisted, making it impossible to audit or replay the staking reward calculation and allowing undetected manipulation of pending_reward values?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/record/RecordFileDownloader.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: getSidecar()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the sidecar.setBytes(null) call at line 157 — which nulls out bytes when isPersistBytes() is false — to ensure that after processing, the raw sidecar bytes containing fabricated staking state changes are unrecoverable, preventing forensic detection of the manipulation while the incorrect pending_reward values remain in entity_stake?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/record/RecordFileDownloader.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: downloadSidecars()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the flatMapIterable(SidecarFile::getRecords) call at line 121 — which flattens all records from all accepted sidecar files into a single stream — to cause a sidecar file with a very large number of CONTRACT_STATE_CHANGE records to overwhelm the multimap, causing memory pressure that leads to OOM errors and incomplete processing of staking reward transactions in the same batch?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/record/RecordFileDownloader.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: download()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the lastStreamFile.set(Optional.of(rf)) call at line 84 — where the lastStreamFile AtomicReference is updated with the cutover service's last record file — to cause a hash chain gap where the importer skips verifying the hash chain between the cutover service's cached file and the next downloaded file, allowing a record file with manipulated staking reward paidStakingRewards fields to be accepted?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/record/RecordFileDownloader.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: shouldDownload()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that shouldDownload() always returns true at line 97 — unlike other downloaders that may have circuit breakers — to cause the record file downloader to continuously process files even when the staking reward account (0.0.800) balance is inconsistent, leading to repeated incorrect pending_reward calculations without any halt condition?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/record/RecordFileDownloader.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: downloadSidecars()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that the sidecar type filter at line 118-119 uses sidecar.getTypes() — which is populated from the record file's sidecar metadata, not from the actual sidecar file content — to declare a sidecar as having no types (empty list), causing acceptedTypes.isEmpty() || sidecar.getTypes().stream().anyMatch(acceptedTypes::contains) to evaluate to true when acceptedTypes is empty, and the sidecar is downloaded and processed regardless of its actual content?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/record/RecordFileDownloader.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: downloadSidecars()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the Flux.fromIterable(recordFile.getSidecars()) at line 117 — which iterates over the sidecar list in order — to cause a sidecar file that should contain staking state changes to be processed after a corrupted sidecar that throws a HashMismatchException, causing the entire Flux to error and all subsequent staking-related sidecars to be dropped?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/record/RecordFileDownloader.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: getSidecar()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the streamFileProvider.get(sidecarFilename) reactive call at line 143 — which fetches the sidecar from cloud storage — to perform a time-of-check-to-time-of-use (TOCTOU) attack by replacing the sidecar file in storage between the hash verification in the record file signature and the actual download, injecting staking state changes that were not part of the consensus-verified record?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/record/RecordFileDownloader.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: downloadSidecars()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the recordFile.getItems().forEach() loop at lines 133-138 — which iterates over all RecordItems and attaches sidecar records by timestamp — to cause a staking reward RecordItem to receive sidecar records from a contract execution that happened at the same nanosecond timestamp, injecting unrelated CONTRACT_STATE_CHANGE data into the staking reward processing pipeline?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/record/RecordFileDownloader.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: onVerified()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that downloadSidecars() is called with the StreamFilename from streamFileData at line 91 — which is derived from the downloaded file's path — to cause a path traversal or filename manipulation that fetches a sidecar file from a different record file's directory, injecting staking state changes from a different block into the current RecordFile's items?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/record/RecordFileDownloader.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: downloadSidecars()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the condition at line 111 — where the early return fires if BOTH isWriteFiles() is false AND isEnabled() is false — to cause a configuration state where isEnabled() is true but the sidecar type filter excludes all types, so sidecars are downloaded but all records are filtered out, leaving staking reward RecordItems with empty sidecar records and causing pending_reward to be calculated without the correct stake balance changes?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/record/RecordFileDownloader.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: getSidecar()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that sidecarFileReader.read() at line 144 populates sidecar.setRecords() with the full list of TransactionSidecarRecords from the proto file — including records for transactions not in the current RecordFile — to inject staking state changes for accounts not involved in any transaction in the current block, causing entity_stake to be updated for accounts that should not have their pending_reward modified?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/record/RecordFileDownloader.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: download()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the interaction between cutoverService.get() at line 82 and the lastStreamFile sync at line 84 — where the lastStreamFile is set to the cutover service's last record file before downloadNextBatch() — to cause the hash chain verifier to accept a record file whose previousHash matches the cutover service's cached file but not the actual last processed file, allowing staking reward transaction records to be injected into the stream?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/record/RecordFileDownloader.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: downloadSidecars()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that the second type filter at line 122 — filter(t -> acceptedTypes.isEmpty() || acceptedTypes.contains(getSidecarType(t))) — is applied after the sidecar file is already downloaded and hash-verified, to cause unnecessary processing of sidecar files that contain only filtered-out types, creating a denial-of-service that delays processing of legitimate staking reward transactions?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/record/RecordFileDownloader.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: getSidecar()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that the HashMismatchException at line 147-149 uses sidecar.getName() — which is derived from the record file metadata — as the filename in the error message, to craft a sidecar name that causes log injection or log confusion, obscuring the detection of a staking reward manipulation attack?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/record/RecordFileDownloader.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: downloadSidecars()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the Flux.block() call at line 127 — which blocks the calling thread until the reactive pipeline completes — to cause a deadlock or thread starvation by making sidecar downloads hang indefinitely, preventing the record file from being processed and causing staking reward transactions to never be committed to the database?
